items.title: Trump Administration Says Surprise Billing Law Is Being ‘Gamed’ by Doctors - The New York Times
items.reason: The item addresses Trump Administration positions on No Surprises Act implementation and enforcement concerns, which directly relates to federal rulemaking and policy interpretation affecting the law's operation.
items.summary: The Trump Administration has raised concerns that healthcare providers are circumventing the No Surprises Act's protections against unexpected medical bills, claiming the surprise billing law is being exploited by doctors. The statement indicates potential enforcement challenges or gaps in the current rule implementation that may prompt regulatory attention or clarification. This development is relevant to revenue-cycle teams as it signals potential shifts in how federal authorities may interpret or enforce balance-billing protections and could lead to new guidance on permissible billing practices.

items.title: Cigna Dealt Three Surprise Billing Losses to Doctors in Court - Bloomberg Law News
items.reason: This item directly addresses federal No Surprises Act litigation involving enforcement of surprise billing protections, with a specific case outcome affecting a major insurer's compliance obligations.
items.summary: Cigna lost three court cases brought by physicians challenging the insurer's surprise billing practices and No Surprises Act compliance. These litigation losses establish judicial precedent on how the federal No Surprises Act's balance-billing protections apply to insurers' claim handling and reimbursement practices. The rulings directly impact Cigna's revenue-cycle operations and claims processing, and signal courts' willingness to enforce No Surprises Act requirements against major insurers. Revenue-cycle teams should monitor these decisions as they clarify legal standards for surprise billing dispute resolution and may influence other carriers' policies and practices.

items.title: CMS appeals court ruling on Medicare Advantage star rating methodology as insurers file lawsuits - HealthExec
items.reason: This item directly addresses regulatory enforcement and litigation involving Medicare Advantage insurers challenging CMS policy, which falls squarely within the payer litigation and enforcement topic.
items.summary: CMS is appealing a court ruling on Medicare Advantage star rating methodology while multiple insurers have filed lawsuits related to the same issue. Star ratings directly impact Medicare Advantage plan performance scores and reimbursement incentives, making this a significant regulatory dispute between CMS and major health insurers. The litigation outcome could affect how insurers are evaluated and compensated under the Medicare Advantage program, with implications for revenue cycle management and network planning.

also.title: CMS appeals court decision behind 2026 MA star ratings recalculations
